title: workspaces — data retention windows
description: Data retention windows per model. Drives the retention jobs in `schedule.json` (workspaces:hard-delete-archived + the compliance module's retention orchestrator). All durations are ISO-8601 (`P30D` = 30 days). Compliance tier drives DSAR + erasure behaviour — see `data-classes.json` for the field-level classification and `compliance.json` for the regime × control mapping.

retention.description: Applications are never auto-deleted. Manual archival requires super_admin + audit trail entry.

Soft-delete is manual (WorkspacePolicy@archive). Hard-delete happens 30d after archival via HardDeleteArchivedWorkspaceJob. `is_system=true` rows are skipped. The 30d window matches the customer-facing 'undo archival' promise in the archived-notification email.
Cascade with Workspace on workspace archival. Independent 7d window on Domain@destroy so DNS operators can rollback within a week.
Cascade with Domain (ON DELETE CASCADE). No independent retention.
Cascade with Workspace. Independent 30d window on Branding@destroy allows a rollback if the wrong profile is deleted.

GDPR Art. 17 right-to-erasure. On DSAR erasure request the row is anonymised in place (email → `erased-<id>@invalid.local`, name → null, avatar → null), cascaded to every User FK. Full hard-delete happens 30d after erasure via the compliance module's PurgeSubjectJob.

complianceHold.description: DPO and legal contacts have a 7-year retention hold — GDPR Art. 30 requires the ROPA (Records of Processing) to preserve the DPO contact for 6 years after the processing ends; 7 years gives us safety margin.

retention.description: Cascade with Workspace. The encrypted `config` blob is purged first — KMS key destruction takes precedence over row deletion (crypto-shredding).

complianceTiers.pii: Personally identifiable information. Subject to DSAR / erasure. Encrypted at rest.
complianceTiers.customer_data: Customer-owned records (workspaces, athletes, teams). Retained for the customer's benefit until archival + retention window.
complianceTiers.operational: Platform infra records. No regulatory hold; retention is set for operational safety (rollback windows).
complianceTiers.regulated_configuration: Configuration or credential material with compliance implications. Requires audit trail + crypto-shredding on delete.
complianceTiers.regulated_health: Health / safeguarding data. Not present on workspaces models but declared here for downstream modules to reference the tier taxonomy.
